title: Need help implementing AI into a bot for analysis and trading?
author: Misha_O
selftext:
I created a bot for market analysis on specific pairs. Everything works great; it's enough for me, but I want more.



My goal is to introduce AI into market analysis trading. I need the bot to have logic.

I've connected DeepSeek for analysis. I need some advice.

comments.author: Jazzlike_Syllabub_91
comments.body: What kind of logic? :) / what kind of advice?

replies.author: Misha_O
replies.body:
I need advice on connecting the bot to the exchange so it can place orders based on data analysis.

The bot receives data that signals a position and should place long/short orders.

comments.author: Jazzlike_Syllabub_91
comments.body: I’ve seen people hook up openclaw and create a brain that way. I usually hook it up to the api and go from there

replies.author: Misha_O
replies.body: Can you recommend a service? I would be grateful.

replies.author: Jazzlike_Syllabub_91
replies.body: I used local llms for mine, but you can use Claude or chagpt… (there’s a few others but those are the big ones)

comments.author: Cold-Pin-345
comments.body: I tried Algo Studio since today. For now it looks great for me!

comments.author: GordonLevinson
comments.body: There’s a good platform to trade crypto with AI

comments.author: nikunjverma11
comments.body: Be careful here, LLMs aren’t “logic,” they’re text generators and they’ll confidently hallucinate signals. Keep DeepSeek for narrative analysis only, but make the actual trading decisions rule based with hard risk limits. I’d spec the strategy and invariants in Traycer then implement in Python and validate with Backtrader or vectorbt plus paper trading before touching real money.

replies.author: Misha_O
replies.body: You're right. My DeepSic does this. It provides an explanation of the data it receives. I couldn't get any logs from it, even though I spent a lot of time. I'd let you test it, but I'm afraid it would be advertising.
